one_time_description: Insurance settlement received during the year (related to building lost to fire)
economic_notes: Cree Nation business entities include Saskatchewan River Business Corporation (SRBC) and Cumberland House Saskatchewan River LP (CHSRLP), operated using modified equity method. CHSRLP operates Chief's Island Community Store. COVID-19 noted as significant event with expected negative impact on future casino-related funding from First Nations Trust and Northern Lights CDC. Committed to $6.5M water treatment plant project funded by ISC and $5.144M new office/daycare centre financed by insurance proceeds and loan.
notes: Fiscal year is 2020 (ended March 31, 2020). Province is Saskatchewan. Two GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: SRBC and CHSRLP. CMHC Section 95 Housing Program is consolidated. Cash deficiency (bank indebtedness) of $185,069 reported as negative cash. Restricted cash includes capital projects ($322,377) and CMHC reserves ($2,658,966). A building used for administration and daycare was lost to fire during the year; insurance settlement of $1,688,936 received as one-time revenue. Promissory notes of $1,502,708 relate to Specific Claim and Agricultural Claim advances from Government of Canada. No schedule of remuneration disclosed. Honouraria of $333,662 reported under expenses by object. Child and Family Services revenue of $782,438 appears as a one-year item with no budget allocation.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
